name: Self-Improving App Navigator
description: Observes Android app screens, stores semantic screen maps, learns reusable app procedures, and replays learned procedures when autonomy is enabled.

toolDeclarations.name: open_demo_recorder_settings
toolDeclarations.description: Open Android Accessibility settings so the user can enable Idan's demo recorder for background workflow learning.

toolDeclarations.name: open_demo_overlay_settings
toolDeclarations.description: Open Android draw-over-apps settings so the user can allow Idan to show floating demo recorder controls over other apps.

toolDeclarations.name: show_demo_overlay
toolDeclarations.description: Show floating PLAY/STOP demo recorder controls over other apps. Requires draw-over-apps permission.

toolDeclarations.name: hide_demo_overlay
toolDeclarations.description: Hide the floating demo recorder controls.

toolDeclarations.name: start_app_demonstration
toolDeclarations.description: Start learning from a user demonstration. Captures the current screen as the beginning of a workflow, then waits for the user to perform the workflow manually.

name.description: Short name for the workflow being demonstrated.

goal.description: What the demonstrated workflow should accomplish.

packageName.description: Optional Android package name. Defaults to the currently observed app.

limit.description: Maximum visible UI elements to include. Defaults to 60.

toolDeclarations.name: finish_app_demonstration
toolDeclarations.description: Finish a user demonstration. Captures the final screen and stores the before/after. If the user described the steps, convert them into a runnable learned procedure.

when.description: Optional condition for when to use this learned procedure.

steps.description: Optional steps the user performed, if known. Supported actions: open_app, tap_text, tap_xy, type_text, press_key, wait.

limit.description: Maximum visible UI elements to include. Defaults to 60.

toolDeclarations.name: observe_current_screen
toolDeclarations.description: Read the current Android accessibility UI hierarchy and save a compact semantic screen model with labels, controls, roles, and bounds.

limit.description: Maximum visible UI elements to include. Defaults to 60.

toolDeclarations.name: capture_visible_text
toolDeclarations.description: Capture readable visible text from the current Android screen using accessibility/UI dump. Use this for app pages that cannot be scraped, such as LinkedIn profiles opened in the app or browser. Can optionally scroll and collect multiple screens.

scrolls.description: Number of downward scrolls to perform after the first capture. Defaults to 0; use 2-5 for profile pages.

packageName.description: Optional Android package to open before capturing, e.g. com.linkedin.android or com.android.chrome.

url.description: Optional URL/deep link to open before capturing. Useful for profile pages that require the app or browser.

waitMs.description: Milliseconds to wait after opening the app or URL before the first UI dump. Defaults to 1800.

returnToIdan.description: Whether to reopen Idan after capture so the user can see the result. Defaults to true.

rememberKey.description: Optional memory key to save captured text under, e.g. linkedin:profile:self.

toolDeclarations.name: connect_linkedin_profile
toolDeclarations.description: Connect/import a user's LinkedIn profile by opening their LinkedIn /in/ URL in the LinkedIn app, reading visible profile text with UI dump, scrolling, and saving it to memory.

url.description: The user's LinkedIn profile URL, usually https://www.linkedin.com/in/profile-slug/.

scrolls.description: Number of downward scrolls to collect profile sections. Defaults to 2 for a fast first import.

rememberKey.description: Optional memory key. Defaults to linkedin:profile:self.

toolDeclarations.name: learn_app_procedure
toolDeclarations.description: Save a reusable procedure for an app after observing or successfully performing a workflow. Use this to let Idan improve over time.

name.description: Short procedure name, e.g. open profile search or dismiss premium popup.

packageName.description: Android package this procedure belongs to, e.g. com.linkedin.android.

when.description: Optional natural-language condition for when to use this procedure.

steps.description: Reusable steps. Supported actions: open_app, tap_text, tap_xy, type_text, press_key, wait.

successSignals.description: Optional text labels that suggest the procedure succeeded.

failureSignals.description: Optional text labels that suggest the procedure failed.

confidence.description: Initial confidence from 0.1 to 0.98. Defaults to 0.7.

toolDeclarations.name: list_learned_app_procedures
toolDeclarations.description: List procedures Idan has learned for apps.

packageName.description: Optional package name to filter by.

toolDeclarations.name: delete_learned_app_procedure
toolDeclarations.description: Delete a learned app procedure from Idan's memory when the user says it was wrong, mistaken, outdated, or should be forgotten.

packageName.description: Optional Android package name for exact deletion.

query.description: Search query for fuzzy deletion when packageName is unknown.

toolDeclarations.name: run_learned_app_procedure
toolDeclarations.description: Run a previously learned app procedure. Requires autonomy to be enabled.

packageName.description: Android package name for the procedure.
